title: Guarantee cleanup with trap on EXIT
description: Register a trap on EXIT (and signals) so temp files, locks, and background jobs are released even when the script errors or is interrupted.
body: Use when a script creates temp files, mounts, locks, or background processes. Do not rm a temp dir only at the happy-path end, since an error or Ctrl-C skips it and leaks. Actions: create temp space with `tmp=$(mktemp -d)`, register `trap 'rm -rf "$tmp"' EXIT` immediately after, and trap INT/TERM if you need to kill children; keep the handler idempotent and quote the paths. Validation signals include the temp dir being gone after both a successful run and a forced failure/Ctrl-C, verified with ls. Risk boundary: an unquoted or empty variable in `rm -rf` can delete the wrong tree; verify the variable is set (rm -rf "${tmp:?}") and escalate before any recursive delete near an unset path.
